business_description.nature_of_business: Commercial construction contractor with operations in carpentry, electrical wiring, plumbing, masonry, painting, project management, and clerical support

coverages_discussed.notes: Policy Number WC-KCB-2025-5580; PCRB experience mod calculated at 0.86 under Large Risk rating plan; Risk ID 491498-1604

positives: Favorable experience modification factor of 0.86 indicates better-than-average loss experience
Large total payroll base of $31,594,850 supporting stable exposure
Significant clerical payroll ($5,010,000) with low expected loss rate (0.195)
concerns: High-hazard construction classifications including masonry (5022) and carpentry (5403) with elevated expected loss rates
2021-2022 policy year had 10 claims totaling $240,694 in incurred losses
Multiple trade exposures (carpentry, electrical, plumbing, masonry, painting) increase loss frequency potential
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Three-year loss history: 2023-2024: 7 claims, $191,231 incurred; 2022-2023: 3 claims, $116,479 incurred; 2021-2022: 10 claims, $240,694 incurred. Total actual primary losses $335,671 and excess losses $212,733.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: PCRB Experience Rating Worksheet for Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. under policy WC-KCB-2025-5580 (2025-08-01 to 2026-08-01), valued as of 2026-03-15. Final Experience Modification Factor calculated at 0.86 under the Large Risk rating plan with the Pennsylvania Compensation Rating Bureau.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Pennsylvania-based commercial construction operation with classified payroll across seven class codes: Carpentry NOC (5403), Electrical Wiring (5190), Plumbing NOC (5183), Masonry NOC (5022), Interior Painting (5474), Project Manager/Superintendent (5606), and Clerical Office Employees NOC (8810). Total payroll of $31,594,850 with total expected losses of $951,902.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Actual loss data across three policy years: 2023-2024 (7 claims, $190,770 paid / $191,231 incurred, $120,455 primary / $70,776 excess); 2022-2023 (3 claims, $115,267 paid / $116,479 incurred, $58,500 primary / $57,979 excess); 2021-2022 (10 claims, $240,694 paid and incurred, $156,716 primary / $83,978 excess). Calculated mod numerator $953,971 over denominator $1,109,268 = 0.8600.
